mainDescription.title: What is an Employer of Record?
paragraphs: An employer of record is a third-party provider that is legally responsible for another organization’s employees. It can be located within the same country as the business it employs workers for or in another country with different employment laws.

The EOR handles employment administration, such as payroll and regulatory compliance, while the client business maintains managerial control of its employees and their work assignments. This arrangement allows employers to expand their operations without being overburdened by the administrative and risk management tasks that may arise when entering a new geographic region.

complianceTrust.title: Stay compliant, reduce risk
intro: We align employment, payroll, and documentation with local labour law in each country where we support younot a one-size template forced across every market.
Your payroll records and contracts are maintained so internal reviews and external audits are straightforward: consistent data, clear employer-of-record documentation, and traceable processes.
bullets: Country-by-country alignment with employment and payroll rules.
Payroll and contracts structured to be audit-ready.
Data handled with GDPR-aware practices and ISO-style security controls.

items.question: What is an Employer of Record (EOR)?
items.answer: An Employer of Record is the legal employer for your workers in a country where you do not have your own entity. We handle contracts, international payroll, taxes and contributions where applicable, and core HR while you manage day-to-day work.

items.question: How quickly can we start hiring with EOR?
items.answer: Most clients onboard first hires in about 48–72 hours after scope is clear far faster than incorporating and standing up payroll locally.

items.question: Do we need a local entity for Employer of Record?
items.answer: No. That is the main point of EOR: hire without entity setup. We employ under our in-country structure so you can enter markets without a subsidiary.

items.question: Which countries do you support?
items.answer: We deliver Employer of Record and global hiring support in 80+ countries, including major European and APAC hubs. Contact us for your specific locations.

items.question: Who manages day-to-day work?
items.answer: You do. The EOR handles legal employment, payroll, and employment compliance execution not how people do their jobs.

items.question: What compliance does the EOR cover?
items.answer: We apply local labour rules, statutory benefits, social contributions, and filing obligations for employed staff in scope so international payroll and HR stay aligned with each jurisdiction.

items.question: Can we switch from another EOR to Jackson & Frank?
items.answer: Yes. We plan transfers, contract continuity, and payroll handoffs so there is no gap for employees.

items.question: Do you offer immigration support with EOR?
items.answer: Yes. We coordinate work permits and visas alongside employment so relocation and global hiring stay in one programme.

cta.title: Start global hiring with Employer of Record
cta.description: 700+ companies use us for international payroll and employment compliance. Most first hires go live in 48–72 hours.
